channel.title: nexusEVnews
channel.description: Independent EV and renewable energy news, curated daily. Electric vehicles, solar, battery tech, and charging infrastructure.

item.title: Hyundai's Ioniq V Debuts in Beijing With a 27-Inch 4K Screen and 600 km of Range
item.dc:creator: OtoFootage / Car and Driver
item.description: The Hyundai Ioniq V is the first dedicated production model for Hyundai's revived Ioniq brand in China, debuting at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show. Built on an 800-volt eGMP platform and derived from the Venus concept, it measures just under 193 inches long on a 2.9-meter wheelbase and is confirmed for over 600 km of range on the CLTC standard. A 27-inch ultra-thin 4K panoramic display spans the dashboard. The interior adds Airhug massage seats, Nebula ambient lighting, Dolby Atmos sound, and rear-wheel-drive and all-wheel-drive powertrain options. Hyundai plans to supply Britain, Europe, and the Middle East from China after a late-2026 production start.

item.title: Tesla's Longer Model Y Has a Functional Third Row and a Strong Case for Family Buyers
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Reviews
item.description: Out of Spec Reviews gets a first look at the Tesla Model Y L in China. The L stretches the standard platform to fit a real third row: at 6'1", Kyle sat in all three rows simultaneously with knee clearance in the back. The top-spec long-range variant carries approximately 84-to-88 kWh of LG cells, dual-motor all-wheel drive, and 250 kW peak DC charging. Second-row seating is captain's chairs only. Adaptive damping and double glazing are standard. The wireless phone charger has been upgraded to 50 watts with active cooling. A US prototype has been spotted.

item.title: A $35,000 Manual Sports Car vs a $55,000 Electric Sedan: Which One Do You Actually Want to Drive?
item.dc:creator: Gjeebs
item.description: Gjeebs drives the Subaru BRZ TS and Tesla Model 3 Performance back to back. BRZ TS: 228 hp, 187 lb-ft, around 2,900 pounds, six-speed manual only, Brembo brakes, Michelin Pilot Sport 4 tires, roughly $35,000. Model 3 Performance: 510 hp, 553 lb-ft, 2.9 seconds to 60, about 300 miles of range, around $55,000, and roughly 1,100 pounds heavier. The BRZ TS is naturally aspirated with no direct competition, good for track days and learning the limits of a car. The Model 3 wins on every daily-use metric. Both are valid at different life stages.

item.title: BMW Brings Three Neue Klasse Premieres to Beijing, Including an i3 With 1,000 km of Range
item.dc:creator: BMW Group
item.description: BMW revealed the iX3 Long Wheelbase, i3 Long Wheelbase, and refreshed 7 Series at Auto China 2026 in Beijing, all built on the Neue Klasse platform. The i3 Long Wheelbase, the second-generation electric 3 Series for China, is rated at over 1,000 km on CLTC. The i7 electric 7 Series carries close to 800 km of CLTC range. A fourth car, the iX3 Flow Edition, uses E-Ink panels on major exterior body components. All vehicles share BMW's panoramic iDrive and Level 2 navigation-guided assistance developed with Chinese AI partner Momenta. BMW has committed to 40 new or updated models by 2027.

item.title: The Ioniq 5 N and the Dodge Charger EV Both Claim to Be Fun. One of Them Actually Is.
item.dc:creator: The Drive
item.description: Kyle and Nick from The Drive take the Hyundai Ioniq 5 N and Dodge Charger Daytona EV to Chuck Walla Raceway and a canyon road. Both cars have over 640 horsepower and all-wheel drive. The Charger weighs approximately 5,800 pounds; the Ioniq 5 N is roughly 1,000 pounds lighter. On track, the Ioniq 5 N was communicative with paddle-operated simulated shifts and adjustable torque vectoring. The Charger showed throttle lag and persistent understeer. Porsche has since begun adding virtual gear shifts to its own EVs after evaluating the Ioniq 5 N.

item.title: The Licensing Business Nobody Saw Coming
item.dc:creator: Jacob Hunka
item.description: Rivian's $5.8 billion deal with Volkswagen has been treated as a one-off. I don't think it is. I see it as the first proof point in a second business Rivian has been quietly building for years, and the next twelve months will show whether it works.

item.title: The New Cayenne Coupé Goes Electric, and the Range Numbers Back Up the Lower Roofline
item.dc:creator: Autogefühl
item.description: The all-new Cayenne Coupé shares the SUV's 4.99-meter length but sits 2.4 centimeters lower, cutting drag from 0.25 to 0.23. The 108 kWh net battery charges at up to 400 kW DC on 800-volt architecture; Porsche's own test with the SUV hit 10 to 80 percent in under 15 minutes. Three trims available: base at 4.8 seconds, S at 3.8 seconds, Turbo at 2.5 seconds 0 to 100 km/h. German pricing runs €109,000 to €169,000, each €4,000 above the equivalent SUV. Dual-side AC charging, optional electric doors that close when the seatbelt is fastened, and a Coupé-exclusive lightweight package saving around 18 kilograms.

item.title: Fourteen Years, 3% of Parts in Common. The Story of How the Tesla Model S Changed Everything.
item.dc:creator: Hagerty
item.description: Tesla has released a final Signature Edition to mark the end of the Model S after 14 years. Jason Cammisa's retrospective for Hagerty puts the production run in perspective: only 3% of parts are shared between the 2012 original and the 2026 car. A conventional automaker carries over around 50% of parts even after a full generation change. The long-range model lost 375 pounds and became roughly 40% more efficient through thousands of rolling changes. The video also compares the Model S directly to the BMW M5 across the same 14-year span, covering speed, weight, efficiency, and price.

item.title: Solar Grew Faster Than Any Other Energy Source Last Year. Renewables Are Now Absorbing All New Demand.
item.dc:creator: CBC News
item.description: The IEA and UK think tank Ember both published reports this week showing solar outpaced every other fuel source from 2024 to 2025, driven by China and India. Global solar output now matches total EU electricity consumption across all 27 member states. Renewables as a group are absorbing all new global electricity demand, meaning no new fossil fuel capacity is required to close the gap. The CBC's Anand Ram also covers Pakistan's estimated $12 billion in avoided costs from solar adoption during the current Iranian oil disruption, with analysis from Jessica Isaacs of the World Resources Institute.

item.title: How China Came to Control Every Critical Link in the Global EV Battery Supply Chain
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Originals
item.description: Bloomberg Originals traces China's dominance across the lithium-ion battery supply chain, from raw material refining to cell production. A 2001 state strategy and the 2015 Made in China 2025 plan designated batteries and EVs as national priorities. Four of the top five global battery makers are Chinese. The film profiles GM's new LMR chemistry targeting 2028 commercial launch for SUVs and pickup trucks, Gotion's first US factory in Manteno, Illinois (currently 300 employees, scaling to 750 by end of 2026), and Norwegian recycler Hydrovolt, which processes around 200 types of used EV batteries into black mass for re-refining.

item.title: This UK Organic Farm Had Solar for Years. The Batteries Are What Made It Work.
item.dc:creator: Victron Energy
item.description: Sheepdrove Organic Farm in Berkshire had over a thousand solar panels, wind turbines, and ground-source heat pumps but kept selling cheap daytime power and buying it back at higher cost in the evening. The solution: nine Victron 15 kVA Quattro inverter chargers totaling 135 kVA, paired with 160 kWh of battery storage. The system follows a defined priority order: power site loads first, charge batteries second, export surplus third. In winter the farm draws around one megawatt hour per day; heat pumps alone account for 300 to 400 kWh. Grid protection uses Modbus communication with Fronius inverters to cap export at 300 amps.

item.title: The R2 Is Rivian's Mass-Market Moment. A $5.8B Software Licensing Deal Hints at What Comes Next.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Technology
item.description: RJ Scaringe tells Bloomberg the R2 is in production with strong early demand. The lineup runs from $45,000 to $57,000, straddling the US average new car price. Point-to-point Level 2 autonomy arrives later in 2026; Level 3 follows in 2027. A second R2 variant at the end of 2026 adds Rivian's in-house 800-TOPS silicon and LiDAR. Scaringe also outlined Rivian's technology licensing strategy: the $5.8 billion VW deal covers Rivian's OS and compute stack, with autonomy licensing described as a longer-term target.

item.title: BMW's Updated 7 Series Has a New Face, 36 Speakers, and a Rear Screen That Streams YouTube
item.dc:creator: Autogefühl
item.description: Autogefühl does a full walkaround of the refreshed BMW 7 Series. The i7 gains 10 kWh for a 112.5 kWh pack, still on 400-volt architecture with 250 kW DC max and 29 minutes from 10 to 80 percent. The 740 xDrive runs a 3-liter inline-6 with 400 horsepower and 5.1 seconds to 100 km/h. The M760e PHEV tops out at 571 horsepower and 4.3 seconds; its PHEV battery is 18.7 kWh with AC charging only. The interior borrows from the Neue Klasse with a Panoramic Vision strip; the updated rear theater screen streams YouTube directly. Pricing starts at 120,000 euros.

item.title: Quebec Has About Half of Canada's EV Charging. A Tour of Autoroute 20 Shows Why.
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Roaming
item.description: Quebec hosts roughly half of all EV charging stations in Canada, powered by hydroelectricity at around 10 cents Canadian per kWh at home. Out of Spec Roaming drives an F-150 Lightning along Autoroute 20, covering Circuit Electric, Tesla, Parkland, Kushtar, and smaller regional networks. Circuit Electric pricing tiers to around 50 cents per kWh; Tesla is the cheapest option at about 47 cents ad hoc or 26 cents on subscription. The largest charging site in Canada is in St-Louis-de-Blandford with 44 chargers. Pull-through capability for trucks and trailers is now standard at provincial rest areas.

item.title: Smart's New City EV Is 2.97 Metres Long, Offers 300 km of Range, and Includes V2L
item.dc:creator: Everything Electric APAC
item.description: The Smart #2 is a compact four-seater on a purpose-designed platform called ECA, developed since 2019. It is 2.97 meters long and 1.9 meters wide, with a 6.95-meter turning circle. Claimed range is 300 km WLTP or 400 km CLTC, with V2L and DC fast charging included. Design was handled by Smart's European studio, reviving the two-tone body language of the original. The production-ready version debuts at Paris in October 2026, with sales expected early next year. A second vehicle shown, the Smart #6 sedan hybrid, is currently planned for China only.

item.title: Oil Prices Are Pushing Consumers Toward EVs. China Is Best Positioned to Benefit.
item.dc:creator: DW News
item.description: DW News examines how oil prices spiking from the Iran conflict are creating new urgency around EV adoption. Germany's EV registrations exceeded gasoline car sales for the first time in March. EU pump prices averaged €1.81 per liter in mid-April. In the US, EV demand dropped sharply after the federal tax credit was eliminated; VW sold just 250 ID4s in the three months after the credit ended and has paused Tennessee production of the model. China produces more than half of all EVs globally, controls over 80 percent of rare earth processing capacity, and Chinese brands hold over 80 percent of Brazil's EV market.

item.title: The 2027 Chevrolet Bolt Charges Three Times Faster Than Its Predecessor. Here's What the Data Shows.
item.dc:creator: State Of Charge
item.description: State Of Charge tests the 2027 Bolt RS at a 350 kW EVgo station. The LFP pack peaks at 158 kW, hits 70 percent in 30 minutes, and completes a full charge in under an hour. The original Bolt needed more than two hours for the same session. GM claims 26 minutes from 10 to 80 percent; this first test on a 22-mile-old battery showed 33.3 minutes, with a follow-up planned. EPA-rated range is 262 miles. Base price starts under $30,000.

item.title: A San Francisco Arcade Operator Fit a Skeeball Machine in the TELO Truck. Done.
item.dc:creator: TELO Trucks
item.description: Joey the Cat runs 40 Bay Area bar locations and moves about 50 skeeball machines and hundreds of arcade games between them. His test for the TELO electric truck: does a skeeball machine fit in the bed? It does, with room left for his tools. He currently hauls in a 2011 Toyota Tacoma and a 2021 Mercedes Sprinter van and wants a smaller electric option that can handle city parking without giving up cargo utility. Episode 8 of TELO's How Will You TELO? series.

item.title: CATL Unveiled Six Battery Technologies at Its 2026 Tech Day. The Headline Numbers Are Striking.
item.dc:creator: Everything Electric APAC
item.description: CATL's Super Technology Day brought the third-generation Shenxing battery at 10 to 80 percent in 3 minutes 44 seconds and 1,000 cycles with above 90 percent capacity retention. The Qilin Condensed Battery reaches 350 Wh/kg and 760 Wh/L for 1,500 km of range in a sedan. The third-generation Qilin LFP hits 280 Wh/kg and 3 MW peak power. The Naxtra sodium-ion battery is moving to large-scale production. CATL also announced 4,000 integrated charge-swap stations covering nearly 190 cities by end of 2026.

item.title: 13 Months and 38,700 Miles in a Used Taycan 4S: The Full Ownership Report
item.dc:creator: Driven Car Reviews
item.description: Tom Voelk bought a 2021 Taycan 4S CPO for $74,000 against a $163,000 MSRP. After 13 months: one software glitch that cleared with a five-minute lock-and-walk-away, several pre-purchase recalls already handled, and strong pack health. After a manual rebalance, indicated range is 215 miles in cold weather and expected to return to around 260 miles in summer. He charges overnight at 9 cents per kWh in Seattle. He would buy it again.
